opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My face feels like it's trapped in a vice and the pressure is absolute agony whenever I bend forward. It started as a normal head cold about five days ago but the last two days it's gone downhill fast — thick discoloured gunk pouring out of my nose, and a deep throbbing ache across my cheeks and forehead and even up into my top teeth. Paracetamol has done basically nothing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain has escalated over the last 48 hours, it's worse every time I lean forward, and the regular painkillers aren't touching it — I want to know what's going on and how to get on top of the pain.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 5 days ago it started as a mild head cold with a blocked nose and clear runny nose
symptom_narrative.duration: 5 days total, but sharply worse over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: thick, discoloured yellow-green discharge now instead of clear; a deep throbbing ache and heavy pressure across the cheeks and forehead; an aching feeling in the upper teeth; a reduced sense of smell; a nagging cough that is worse at night from drip down the back of the throat
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the facial pressure is agonising, especially bending forward — the worst part; ordinary paracetamol has had almost no effect
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: across both cheeks and the forehead, and up into the upper teeth
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant pressure that spikes sharply on leaning or bending forward
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much so far; sitting upright is a little better than bending
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: bending forward, leaning down, lying flat

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally well, no ongoing health problems

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: paracetamol 500mg taken a couple of times with almost no effect

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with partner; independent day-to-day but currently laid up by the positional facial pain

objective_data_offered.value: temperature around 37.6 on a home thermometer
thick yellow-green discharge coming from both nostrils
visibly uncomfortable, holding and guarding the face, moving the head cautiously and reluctant to bend forward on camera
no swelling or redness around either eye, no double vision and vision feels normal when asked
